questions.question: A circle has center (3, −2) and passes through the point (7, 1). Write the equation of the circle in standard form.
questions.explanation: Find the radius using the distance formula: r = √[(7−3)² + (1−(−2))²] = √[16 + 9] = √25 = 5. The standard form is (x − h)² + (y − k)² = r², so (x − 3)² + (y + 2)² = 25.

questions.question: A tangent line to the circle x² + y² = 13 has slope 2. Find the y-intercept of one such tangent line.
questions.explanation: A line y = 2x + b is tangent to x² + y² = 13 when the distance from center (0,0) to the line equals the radius √13. Distance = |b|/√5. Set |b|/√5 = √13, so |b| = √65. The tangent lines are y = 2x + √65 and y = 2x − √65.

questions.question: A sector of a circle with radius 6 has a central angle of 2π/3 radians. Find the area of the sector.
The circle (x − 1)² + (y − 4)² = 32 intersects the line y = x + 2. How many points of intersection are there?
questions.explanation: Substitute y = x + 2 into the circle equation: (x − 1)² + (x + 2 − 4)² = 32 simplifies to (x − 1)² + (x − 2)² = 32, or 2x² − 6x + 5 = 32, giving 2x² − 6x − 27 = 0. The discriminant is 36 + 216 = 252 > 0, so there are 2 distinct real solutions and thus 2 intersection points.

questions.question: An arc on a circle with radius 9 has arc length 3π. Find the measure of the central angle in radians.
questions.explanation: Arc length s = rθ, where θ is in radians. So 3π = 9θ, giving θ = 3π/9 = π/3 radians.

questions.question: A circle passes through the points (0, 0), (8, 0), and (0, 6). Find the radius of the circle.
questions.explanation: The center of a circle through (0,0), (8,0), and (0,6) is equidistant from all three. By symmetry, the center lies on the perpendicular bisectors. The center is (4, 3). Radius = √(4² + 3²) = √(16 + 9) = √25 = 5.
